dateline: Johnson Space Center, Houston / Kennedy Space Center — 2017–Present
heroHeadline: Orion Heat Shield Inspection Week 2; NASA Confirms Better-Than-Expected Artemis II Performance
heroSubtitle: Apr 22: Orion is in KSC's Multi-Payload Processing Facility for its formal 30-day heat shield inspection, now in week two. NASA's Apr 20 initial assessments confirm the Avcoat material performed 'significantly better' than Artemis I under the modified skip-reentry trajectory — a critical finding for Artemis IV's 2028 crewed lunar landing. SpaceX Starship V3 (Flight 12) cleared full-stack static fires; late-April/early-May orbital launch from Starbase Pad 2 advances HLS qualification path.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
